frameworks.name: Bayesian optimization
frameworks.one_line: sequential global optimization of an unknown function using a probabilistic surrogate.

frameworks.name: Gaussian-process surrogate with smoothness prior
frameworks.one_line: GP prior whose smoothness assumption can be misspecified relative to the true target.

core_question: How does misspecifying prior smoothness assumptions about the target function affect Bayesian optimization performance?
key_assumptions: target function has some true smoothness
optimizer assumes a (possibly wrong) smoothness prior
hyper-parameters may or may not be optimized online
manipulations: degree of mismatch between assumed and true smoothness
key_findings: the regret penalty grows with input dimension
the penalty persists even when hyper-parameters are optimized
interpretations: prior smoothness mismatch is a substantive failure mode of Bayesian optimization, not removable by hyper-parameter tuning.
